pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the Zod repository, explaining what Zod is, its purpose as a TypeScript-first schema validation library, and provide a high-level overview of the repository structure and key features

page_notes.content: should be highly detailed with erd diagrams for each part and responsibility

pages.title: Core Architecture
pages.purpose: Deep dive into the fundamental architecture of Zod, covering the schema system, validation engine, and how they work together to provide runtime validation and static type inference

pages.title: Schema System
pages.purpose: Explain the core schema system, including how schemas are defined, the $ZodType interface, primitive and complex types, and the internal structure of schema instances

pages.title: Validation Engine
pages.purpose: Document the validation pipeline, including the parse/safeParse methods, synchronous vs asynchronous validation, check execution, and error generation

pages.title: Type System and Inference
pages.purpose: Explain how Zod bridges TypeScript's static type system with runtime validation, covering z.infer, z.input, z.output, and the dual-layer type safety architecture

pages.title: API Variants
pages.purpose: Overview of the three different API surfaces Zod provides (Classic, Mini, and v3 Legacy) and guidance on which to use

pages.title: Classic API
pages.purpose: Document the method-chaining Classic API (z.string().min(5).email()), which is the default and most feature-rich interface

pages.title: Mini API
pages.purpose: Document the functional, tree-shakable Mini API, explaining its design philosophy, usage patterns, and when to choose it over Classic

pages.title: V3 Legacy API
pages.purpose: Explain the v3 API maintained for backward compatibility, migration path from v3 to v4, and breaking changes

pages.title: Advanced Features
pages.purpose: Overview of advanced Zod features including codecs, error customization, JSON Schema conversion, and metadata

pages.title: Codecs and Bidirectional Transforms
pages.purpose: Document the codec system for bidirectional transformations, including encode/decode operations, type safety in transformations, and use cases

pages.title: Error Customization and Localization
pages.purpose: Explain error customization mechanisms (schema-level, per-parse, global), the localization system with 40+ languages, and error precedence chain

pages.title: JSON Schema Conversion
pages.purpose: Document the toJSONSchema() system, including the conversion pipeline, handling of different schema types, cycles and reuse, and configuration options

pages.title: Metadata and Registries
pages.purpose: Explain the metadata system, global and custom registries, and how to attach descriptions, examples, and custom metadata to schemas

pages.title: Version Management
pages.purpose: Explain Zod's versioning strategy, including subpath exports, backward compatibility approach, and the v4 release

pages.title: Versioning Strategy
pages.purpose: Document the subpath versioning approach (zod/v3, zod/v4, zod/v4/mini), shared core types, and how library authors can depend on Zod

pages.title: V4 Release Notes
pages.purpose: Comprehensive documentation of Zod v4 features, performance improvements (14x faster parsing), breaking changes, and migration guide

pages.title: Repository Structure and Build System
pages.purpose: Explain the monorepo structure, package organization, and how the build system works

pages.title: Monorepo Organization
pages.purpose: Document the pnpm workspace structure, packages within the monorepo (zod, docs, bench, treeshake, resolution, tsc), and their relationships

pages.title: Build Pipeline
pages.purpose: Explain the build process using zshy compiler, generation of multiple module formats (ESM, CJS, types), and the postbuild stub generation

pages.title: CI/CD and Release Automation
pages.purpose: Document the GitHub Actions workflows for testing, building, and releasing to npm and JSR, including normal releases vs canary releases

pages.title: Testing and Quality Assurance
pages.purpose: Overview of the testing strategy, including unit tests, benchmarking, and bundle size analysis

pages.title: Test Suite
pages.purpose: Document the test structure, testing patterns for schemas and validation, and how tests are organized across API variants

pages.title: Benchmarking System
pages.purpose: Explain the benchmarking suite in packages/bench, comparison methodology across Zod versions and competitor libraries, and performance metrics

pages.title: Ecosystem and Integration
pages.purpose: Overview of the Zod ecosystem, including community integrations, how to integrate Zod into libraries, and the ecosystem documentation

pages.title: Ecosystem Overview
pages.purpose: Catalog of 70+ ecosystem resources including API libraries (tRPC, oRPC), form integrations, converters, mocking libraries, and utilities

pages.title: Library Authors Guide
pages.purpose: Comprehensive guide for library authors on integrating Zod, including peer dependency configuration, supporting multiple Zod versions, and accepting user-defined schemas

pages.title: Contributing to Zod
pages.purpose: Guide for contributors covering development setup, contribution workflow, coding standards, and how to submit pull requests
